Now who are we marketing this to? This is a former clients that at the notes here is to exclude people from the last 90 days. And the reason for that is that it should play. Your lead list with this as well but in the test that we ran we ran to just former clients and it worked really well. Leads and so especially with former clients the schedule rate, show rate, etc. tend to be really high especially like the show rate for former clients because nice thing is that if you think about it from a customer in terms of an acquisition standpoint it increases the lifetime value of your entire regular basis because then it means that whenever you cancel let's say you can always recover 25% of the people who cancel through reactivation then it means if you're currently working on a 10% turn which I'm not saying is good but I'm saying let's say you did it you had a 10% turn month over month but you always have reconverted 25% then you'd actually have a realistic turn of 7.5% because 25% of those people come back. Alright so it actually has a pretty big overall effect on the business from a lifetime value standpoint.
